title: Yang-Mills Instantons, Quivers and Bows
speaker: Sergey Cherkis
affiliation: University of Arizona; Member, School of Mathematics
abstract: The study of hyperkaehler manifolds of lowest dimension (and of gauge theory on them) leads to a chain of generalizations of the notion of a quiver: quivers, bows, slings, and monowalls. This talk focuses on bows, their representations, and solutions. In particular, we formulate a complete construction of Yang-Mills instantons on Asymptotically Locally Flat (ALF) spaces.

proof_stages.label: Moment-map and Hopf-coordinate local model
proof_stages.summary: The talk builds circle-moment-map coordinates first on the complex plane and then on the quaternionic line, identifying the Hopf fibration, the metric in adapted coordinates, and the Taub-NUT deformation preserving the hyperkähler structure.

proof_stages.label: Multi-Taub-NUT ALF geometry and topology
proof_stages.summary: The Gibbons-Hawking multi-center metric is introduced as a smooth ALF hyperkähler space whose apparent singularities are nuts, whose infinity is a Hopf fibration of degree k, and whose compactification yields cigar spheres, interval cycles, and homology/lattice data.

proof_stages.label: Asymptotic-growth hierarchy and bow target
proof_stages.summary: Multi-Taub-NUT spaces are placed in the ALE/ALF/ALG/ALH hierarchy, where quivers, bows, slings, and monowalls provide corresponding lower-dimensional moduli descriptions, motivating bows as the ALF analogue relevant to instantons.

proof_stages.label: ASD instanton analytic setup and asymptotic theorem
proof_stages.summary: On a fixed ALF space an instanton is defined as a finite-energy anti-self-dual unitary connection, and the needed analytic theorem gives curvature decay, direction-independent limiting circle holonomy, and an eigenline splitting at infinity under generic asymptotics.

proof_stages.label: Fixed topological and boundary data
proof_stages.summary: The asymptotic splitting and the compactified two-cycles define the monopole charges, limiting holonomy parameters, Chern numbers on cigar cycles, Young-diagram constraints, and instanton number that label the moduli problem, with analogous flag data in non-generic cases.

proof_stages.label: Dirac/Nahm transform to bow data
proof_stages.summary: Twisting the given instanton by tautological abelian instantons produces a family of Dirac operators whose index bundle over a circle has prescribed jumps, and projecting coordinate multiplication to the kernels yields a connection and three endomorphisms satisfying Nahm-type equations.

proof_stages.label: Bow moduli equivalence and inverse construction
proof_stages.summary: A bow is encoded by intervals, arrows, marked data, bundles, and hyperkähler reduction, and the main result identifies the bow-solution moduli space isometrically with the instanton moduli space, with an explicit inverse transform and related algebraic-geometric formulations.
